message:
Fix variable reassignment bug with scope tracking

CRITICAL FIX: Variables can now be reassigned without shadowing errors.

Bug: PyAOT treated every assignment as a new declaration (const/var),
causing "local constant shadows outer scope" errors when reassigning
variables in loops.

Example (was broken):
```python
sum = 0
for i in range(10):
    sum = sum + 1  # ❌ Generated: const sum = sum + 1 (shadowing!)
print(sum)  # Printed 0 instead of 10
```

Now works:
```python
sum = 0
for i in range(10):
    sum = sum + 1  # ✅ Generated: sum = sum + 1 (reassignment!)
print(sum)  # Prints 10 ✅
```

Changes:
- Add scope tracking to NativeCodegen (stack of StringHashMaps)
- New methods: pushScope(), popScope(), isDeclared(), declareVar()
- Update genAssign() to check isDeclared() before emitting
  - First assignment: var x = value (mark as declared)
  - Reassignment: x = value (no var/const keyword)
- Add scope push/pop to all loops and functions
- Fix symbol lookup: "pyaot_main" → "main"
- Smart const/var heuristic for first assignments

Test results:
- Before: 8/89 passing (9%)
- After: 14/89 passing (16%)
- Critical fixes: for_loop.py, fibonacci.py now work

Files modified:
- src/codegen/native/main.zig (+48 lines)
- src/codegen/native/statements.zig (+98 lines, -30 refactored)
- src/main.zig (symbol lookup fix)

Implemented by: 3 parallel agents (investigation, scope tracking, genAssign fix)
